notes.anchor: was never in the Balearics at all
notes.note: The Balearic Islands (Majorca, Minorca, Ibiza) off the Spanish coast; the rumour-mill placed the fugitive Pompeians there after the defeat at Thapsus. The Pompey meant here is the dead general's elder son, Gnaeus Pompeius, who in fact reached Spain.
Paciaecus (L. Junius Paciaecus) was a Spaniard of Roman family with connections in the peninsula, here cited as the source of war news from the West. He served Caesar in the Spanish campaign.

Praeneste (modern Palestrina), a hill town southeast of Rome famous for its great sanctuary of Fortuna, was a fashionable resort. Cicero notes wryly that the Caesarian smart set is amusing itself there while the African war is still being decided.

L. Cornelius Balbus the elder, a Gades-born financier and Caesar's confidential agent; his lavish building is Cicero's shorthand for the new men profiting under the regime. The Greek tag that follows means "what does he care?"
